meta.titleTemplate: Import Coconut Charcoal to the USA: HTS, Duty, FDA
meta.descriptionTemplate: Importing UN 1361 coconut charcoal to the USA: HTS classification, the current duty stack, ISF 10+2 entry, and the FDA position on hookah charcoal.

h1: Importing coconut charcoal to the USA
heroSubtitle: The US import mechanics for UN 1361 coconut charcoal from {{port}}: how it is classified, the duties and fees, the entry process, and the FDA position on hookah charcoal.

hero.ctaQuoteAria: Ask about US import on WhatsApp

intro.p1: This page sits under the {{brand}} Logistics pillar and is the template for our import-by-country guides. It covers the customs mechanics; the commercial case for the US market lives on the market page.

keyFacts.heading: US import at a glance
keyFacts.definition: Classification, headline duty, and who clears the cargo.
keyFacts.htsValue: Candidates under heading 4402 (both Free base duty)
keyFacts.dutyValue: MFN Free + a Section 122 surcharge (≈10%, contested)
keyFacts.fdaValue: Hookah charcoal is a regulated tobacco-product component

hts.body: Coconut shell charcoal falls under HTS heading 4402. Two 10-digit codes are candidates; both carry a Free base (MFN) duty, so the exact code does not change the base rate. A CBP binding-ruling request would settle it — confirm classification with your customs broker.

duty.body: US duty on heading 4402 is a layered stack, and the reciprocal-tariff layer is in active legal flux. Read it as a dated stack with a legal-status flag, not a single number.

landed.body: Landed cost is the sum of the goods, the freight, and the import charges. The structure below holds for any lane; the duty components are public verified figures, while the FOB and freight are illustrative and quoted at booking.
landed.fobValue: Illustrative only — your actual FOB applies
landed.dutyValue: From the duty stack above (MFN Free + Section 122 + MPF + HMF)
landed.note: No charcoal per-ton price is published. The formula is shown so you can plug in your own figures; we quote the live numbers at booking.
landed.rulesLinkLabel: What your incoterm covers (FOB and the freight split)

entry.body: US entry for a UN 1361 container follows the standard CBP sequence, handled by the buyer's customs broker with data we supply.

dgHandling.body: The cargo arrives as declared UN 1361, Class {{unClass}} dangerous goods, and is handled accordingly at the US port. The classification and the prevention controls are covered on the dangerous-goods pages.

fda.h2: FDA: hookah charcoal as a tobacco-product component
fda.intro: This is regulatory information, not legal advice — see the disclaimer below.
fda.practicalBody: Work with US counsel and a customs broker experienced in tobacco-product entries before you ship; confirm registration, listing, and marketing-authorization status for your specific product.

ports.body: Indicative ocean transit from {{portWithCode}} to the main US gateways:
ports.empty: US transit times available on request.

items.q: What is the HTS code for coconut charcoal?
items.a: It falls under HTS heading 4402. Two 10-digit codes are candidates — 4402.20.00.00 ("of shell or nut") and 4402.90.01.00 ("other") — and both carry a Free base duty, so the exact code does not change the base rate. A CBP binding ruling would settle it; confirm with your broker.

items.q: What duty or tariff applies in the USA?
items.a: The MFN base duty is Free. On top sits a reciprocal layer that is in legal flux — currently a Section 122 surcharge reported at about 10%, which a court struck down (party-specific, on appeal) and which is set to sunset around July 2026. Plus MPF and HMF. Treat it as a dated stack and confirm the live rate with your broker at booking.

items.q: Do I need a customs broker?
items.a: Yes. Customs clearance is the importer's responsibility, handled by a licensed US customs broker. They file the ISF 10+2, post the customs bond, and submit the entry summary (CBP Form 7501) using data we supply.

items.q: Which US ports do you ship to?
items.a: We model the lane ex-{{portWithCode}} to the main US gateways — see the transit list above. Other US ports are available; confirm routing with the carrier at booking.

items.q: Is hookah charcoal FDA-regulated / do I need a PMTA?
items.a: Hookah charcoal is regulated by the FDA as a component or part of a tobacco product when it maintains combustion of waterpipe tobacco. That brings establishment registration, product listing, and a premarket tobacco product application (PMTA) into scope. This is general information — confirm your obligations with US counsel.

The FDA has not adopted a broad enforcement-discretion policy for hookah charcoal. A pending application is not a safe harbor, the posture is not codified, and it can change without notice. Import Alert 98-06 is the live enforcement mechanism, under which CBP may detain or refuse entry.
